meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build high-converting user onboarding to overcome a lack of portfolio. An advanced-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for freelancers.
content.answer_block: Transform your lack of portfolio into a competitive advantage by building a sophisticated, high-friction user onboarding flow. By demonstrating your ability to engineer complex user journeys, you prove your value as a SaaS architect, effectively replacing static design samples with functional, high-converting product logic that clients crave.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, a client's deadline looming, and a portfolio that feels tragically empty. The imposter syndrome is real, but the real issue isn't your talent—it's your positioning. Clients don't buy portfolios; they buy the ability to solve their hardest technical problems, starting with the first touchpoint: user onboarding.

In this guide, we pivot from chasing design trends to architecting high-conversion product systems. We will use Antigravity and Claude Code to build a sophisticated onboarding flow that proves your engineering prowess. This isn't just a UI; it's a measurable business asset that transforms your 'no portfolio' status into a professional case study of technical execution.

steps.title: Define the User Journey Map
steps.description: Before writing code, architect the psychological flow. We need to identify the 'Aha!' moment and map the data collection points required to get there.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead SaaS Architect. Design a 4-step user onboarding flow for a B2B SaaS platform that prioritizes time-to-value. Output a JSON schema mapping the state transitions and required input validations for each step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a directory structure for an onboarding module using Next.js and Tailwind CSS'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Use a robust state management approach to handle user progression. This demonstrates your ability to write scalable, maintainable code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React state machine hook for a 4-step onboarding process. Include persistence logic for local storage and error handling for invalid transitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a custom useOnboarding hook using XState pattern in TypeScript'

steps.title: Implement High-Conversion UI Components
steps.description: Build modular, reusable components that look professional and function flawlessly. Focus on micro-interactions that signal quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable Progress Stepper component and an Input Field wrapper with integrated Zod validation schema support.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create components/onboarding/Stepper.tsx and components/ui/ValidatedInput.tsx with Framer Motion animations'

steps.title: Integrate Backend Data Persistence
steps.description: Connect the frontend to a mock or real backend to show you understand the full lifecycle of user data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure API route handler to persist onboarding progress to a PostgreSQL database using Prisma.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a Prisma schema and a Next.js API route for saving onboarding step progress'

steps.title: Optimize for Analytics and Tracking
steps.description: Ensure every step is trackable. Clients love data-driven developers who build systems that can be optimized later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add an analytics middleware to the onboarding flow that tracks conversion rates at each step using Segment or PostHog events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate PostHog tracking into the onboarding step transition logic'

pro_tips: Always build onboarding as a modular, standalone package that can be dropped into any client project; it increases your reuse value.
Use Zod for schema validation on the client and server to ensure data integrity without writing repetitive error handling boilerplate.
Implement 'optimistic UI updates' for onboarding steps to make the app feel instantaneous, regardless of network latency.
Include a 'skip' option for power users; it shows you understand user persona diversity and reduces friction.
Document your code with JSDoc comments; it makes your repo look like a professional library, which acts as a secondary portfolio piece.
content.next_steps: Once the onboarding flow is live, record a 60-second screen capture of the flow and add it to your GitHub README as a video demonstration. This is more powerful than a static screenshot.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect project to fall into your lap. Build the systems that prove your expertise and start landing high-ticket clients today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
